concerns.text: Test tasks T009, T010, and T011 are listed BEFORE their implementation counterparts (T012-T018). T009 depends on T018 (cleaned CSV), T010 on T014 (imputation logic), and T011 on T016 (normalization). The current ordering implies tests run before the code exists, violating the 'Producer before Consumer' dependency rule. Tests must be listed after the implementation tasks they validate.

Test tasks T019 and T020 are listed BEFORE implementation tasks T021-T027. T019 verifies CV splits (T021/T022) and T020 verifies CPU constraints (T022/T023). These tests cannot execute without the implementation artifacts. Reorder to place implementation tasks before their corresponding test tasks within the User Story block.

Test tasks T028 and T029 are listed BEFORE implementation tasks T030-T036. T028 tests Permutation Importance (T032) and T029 tests Bootstrap logic (T033b). The ordering violates the producer-consumer dependency; the implementation must precede the test in the task list to reflect the execution flow.

Task T015b ('filter out rows with zero porosity variance') is listed after T015 ('detect Degenerate Dataset'). If T015 correctly flags a degenerate dataset and raises an error (as per spec edge cases), T015b is unreachable or redundant. If T015 only flags, T015b should be part of the same logical step or T015 should be split to ensure the filtering logic is clearly defined before the 'detect' step concludes the flow.
